title: Fleet Docker prebakes
description: Declarative, LAYERED set of Depot-built prebaked base images the fleet maintains. Bases are named by TOOLCHAIN (gcc -> c-base, cargo -> rust-base, ...), NEVER by output: ELF / Mach-O / PE / wasm are build outputs, not bases. Each layer adds exactly one toolchain family on top of a parent (`from`), and a builder FROMs the thinnest base that has what it needs. Drives the prebake build (build each layer + push to the org registry) and the downstream `FROM` references. Reviewed as data, not scattered across workflows.

registry.description: Depot org registry images are pushed to / pulled from. Runners are pre-authed (org config).

prebakes.description: Each prebaked base image, ordered bottom-up (a `from` always points at an earlier entry or an external image).

name.description: Image name (registry path is `<registry>/<name>`). Toolchain-named, not output-named.

status.description: `active` = built + pushed today; `planned` = designed, not yet built.

from.description: Parent image this layer builds on: another prebake `name`, or an external base (e.g. `ubuntu:24.04`).

vendorSource.description: When this layer is built from a VENDORED upstream recipe (untracked-by-default) instead of FROM-ing a third-party published image — supply-chain policy: we build + size-optimize our own. Names the upstream (e.g. `platformatic/node-caged`, `emscripten/emsdk`) and the vendored path.

dockerfile.description: Wheelhouse-relative path to the Dockerfile that builds this layer. Built centrally (wheelhouse-owned infra), not cascaded to members.

installs.description: Toolchains/packages THIS layer adds on top of `from` (not the inherited set). Empty array = a pure cache-isolation layer.

libc.description: libc variants built (`glibc`, `musl`). musl is for the static SEA pipeline; most language bases are glibc-only.

platforms.description: Target platforms (Docker `os/arch`). macOS/native builds use Depot macOS runners + a setup-action toolchain, NOT an image.

tagFrom.description: Source of the content hash that decides when to rebuild this layer (a pin set / apt manifest / workflow output).

project.description: Depot build-cache project id, if this layer is built under a specific project. Shared bases may be unset (project TBD).

consumers.description: Repos / builders that FROM this base, in `repo:builder` form (or a repo name for whole-repo CI).

purpose.description: Why this layer exists and what lands on it.
